description_long: This object is a tall, rectangular stone block constructed from a series of stacked, dark gray and blue-toned bricks. The block features a slightly wider base and top, with the central section composed of evenly sized, rectangular stone bricks that are tightly fitted together. The surface of the stones is rough and matte, with visible texturing and subtle color variations that suggest weathering and age. The block appears solid and heavy, with no visible openings, engravings, or decorative elements. Its design is utilitarian, likely intended to serve as a structural or barrier element in an urban or architectural setting. The overall appearance is robust and functional, suitable for use as a support column, boundary marker, or obstacle barrier.
description: A tall, rectangular stone block made of stacked, rough-textured dark gray and blue bricks with a wider base and top. The surface is matte and weathered, giving it a sturdy, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full height and width of the stone block, highlighting the stacked brick pattern and the slightly protruding base and top.
description_view_1: Side view is visually identical to the front, displaying the same brick arrangement and proportions.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, with the same stone texture and blocky structure visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view is also identical, showing the same stacked stone pattern and proportions.
attribute_location_context: urban retaining wall or boundary in a city park or sidewalk
attribute_installation_method: mortared in place or set atop a concrete foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: Subtle surface weathering with slight discoloration and roughness, but no major chips or cracks visible.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous wall or column
